home.hero_body: AI lesson notes, whiteboard, resource library, parent portal, and payments — all in one place. Free to start, 0% commission.

home.features_heading: Everything your practice needs. Nothing it doesn't.

features.title: AI lesson notes
features.body: After each session, AI drafts your notes — topics covered, difficulty, homework. Review, edit, and share with students in seconds.

features.title: Collaborative whiteboard
features.body: A shared digital canvas built into every lesson. Sketch, annotate, and work through problems side by side in real time.

features.title: Resource library
features.body: Upload worksheets, past papers, and notes once. Share them with individual students or your whole practice — no email attachments.

features.title: Parent portal
features.body: Parents see their child's lesson notes, homework, and progress. They subscribe directly — extra visibility that runs itself.

features.title: 0% commission
features.body: fair-do is a flat monthly subscription. We take nothing from your lesson fees — ever. Not 10%, not 25%, zero.

features.title: Group lessons
features.body: Run up to 10 students in one session. One video room, one set of AI notes, individual invoices per student.

features.title: Gift vouchers
features.body: Students can gift your lessons to others. New business that finds you without ads or marketplace fees.

features.title: Smart scheduling
features.body: Your calendar, private video rooms, and reminder emails — all set up from the moment you book a lesson.

features.title: Automatic payments
features.body: Students pay by card; money lands in your bank two days after each lesson. No chasing, no invoices, no spreadsheets.

home.savings_body: Tutoring platforms take 10–25% of every lesson. On a typical £35/hr practice with 15 students doing one lesson a week, that's over £500 a month leaving your pocket. fair-do charges a flat £29/month — and nothing from your lessons.

steps.title: Get paid

footer.tagline: The simple way for tutors to run their studio — your students, scheduling, secure video and payments in one place.

items.a: fair-do is software for independent tutors. You bring your own students and run your tutoring your way — fair-do gives you scheduling, secure video lessons, and card payments in one place. It's a tool you subscribe to, not a marketplace that finds students for you.

fair-do is a monthly subscription. Free is free, Pro is around £29/month, and School is for teams. On top of the plan, there's a small commission on card payments to cover processing. Full, current details are on our pricing page.
Yes. The tutoring relationship and your student records belong to you. fair-do is the software and payment processor you use to run your tutoring — we don't own, route, or reassign your students.

Yes. fair-do is built around UK GDPR. For your student data, you are the data controller and fair-do acts as your processor. We use trusted infrastructure providers to run the service, and some sub-processors are based in the US under Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs). Our privacy notice sets out exactly who we use and why.
fair-do is for qualified tutors. During onboarding we ask for teaching qualifications (such as QTS or a PGCE) and a current DBS check, and verify the details before a profile goes live.

items.q: How do I get set up?
items.a: Create an account, choose "I am a tutor", and complete onboarding — your profile, qualification details, availability, and connecting your bank via Stripe so you can take card payments. Once your details are verified you're ready to schedule students and run lessons.

Students pay by card through Stripe. Money goes to your connected account and typically reaches your bank around 2 business days after a lesson. fair-do's own charge is your monthly plan plus a small card commission — see pricing for the details.

about.lead: We build software. But we built fair-do because something in this industry felt broken — and we couldn't look away.
about.p1: Good tuition works. The evidence is overwhelming, and most of us have felt it ourselves or watched it change someone we love. We wanted to make it a little easier to reach — and a lot fairer for the people who provide it.
about.p2: Tutors train for years and put real care into the work. We think they should be paid well enough to focus on it — not lose a slice of every lesson to a platform. So we built fair-do as fair, simple software tutors can afford: a flat monthly subscription, never a cut of your teaching fee.

beliefs.title: Tutors should earn enough to focus on teaching
beliefs.body: Not juggle three platforms, not burn out. fair-do is one affordable tool to run the whole thing — a flat monthly plan, not a cut of your lessons.

beliefs.title: Good tuition should reach more people
beliefs.body: Lower fees aren't a marketing trick — they're the point. When lessons cost less, more families who need help can actually get it.

beliefs.title: No company should get rich off the work tutors do
beliefs.body: We take a small, transparent, fixed fee to keep the lights on — and nothing more. A tutor's hard work is not a margin to be optimised.

about.trust_body_pre: Every tutor on fair-do shares their
about.trust_body_strong: teaching qualifications (such as QTS or a PGCE) and a current DBS check
about.trust_body_post: . We check these before a profile goes live, and keep a record of each check.
about.data_body_pre: Your details, and your child's, deserve real care. Lessons run over encrypted video. We store only what's necessary, never sell it, and never use your information to sell ads. See our
about.hello_body: We're a small team and we read everything.

faq_items.q: Do I have to pay to start?
faq_items.a: No. The Free plan runs your whole tutoring business. You only pay if you choose a plan with extra tools.

No. We take no commission on your lessons — you keep what you charge. You pay a flat monthly plan for the software; the only per-lesson cost is Stripe's standard card-processing fee, which we keep none of.
Yes. No lock-in. If you cancel a paid plan it simply runs to the end of the period, then drops to Free — your students and records stay with you.
Always. You own the relationship and the records. fair-do is your tool, never a middleman.

styles.lead: You don't need it all worked out before you start — a good tutor will guide you. Start with the simple version below, and open “Go deeper” on any subject to see what you'll cover and how lessons run.
styles.go_deeper: Go deeper — what you'll cover & how lessons work
styles.unsure_body: Still unsure which subject or level you need? That's completely normal. Answer one short question and we'll match you with tutors who fit — you can always switch.
styles.help_pre: Have a question first? See our

subjects.name: Maths
subjects.what: From times tables to A-level calculus — building confidence step by step, filling the gaps and making the reasoning click rather than just memorising rules.
subjects.feels: Active and worked-through. You'll tackle problems together, with the tutor spotting exactly where it breaks down and setting a little practice between lessons.
subjects.origin: Number, algebra, geometry, ratio, statistics and probability — scaled to your stage, whether that's shoring up KS2 basics or stretching toward A-level.
subjects.history: Lessons map to the national curriculum and the main exam boards (AQA, Edexcel, OCR), so what you practise lines up with what you'll actually be assessed on.
subjects.howItWorks: The tutor checks where you are, targets the gaps, and works through past-paper-style questions until the method feels automatic — building toward steady, visible progress.

subjects.name: English
subjects.what: Reading, writing, grammar and analysis — for everything from early literacy to GCSE literature essays and university applications.
subjects.feels: A mix of discussion and practice. You'll read together, plan and redraft writing, and learn to back up a point with evidence.
subjects.origin: Reading comprehension, creative and persuasive writing, grammar and punctuation, and close analysis of set texts and unseen passages.
subjects.history: Aligned to the national curriculum and the major boards (AQA, Edexcel, OCR), covering both the language and literature papers.
subjects.howItWorks: The tutor builds from where your confidence is, models what good writing looks like, then hands the pen back to you with focused feedback each lesson.

subjects.name: The sciences
subjects.what: Biology, chemistry and physics made concrete — turning abstract ideas into things you can picture, explain and apply under exam conditions.
subjects.feels: Explanatory and hands-on with examples. Expect diagrams, worked calculations and plenty of "but why does that happen?".
subjects.origin: Cells, forces, reactions, energy, the required practicals, and the maths skills that sit underneath every science paper.
subjects.history: Mapped to GCSE Combined and Triple Science and A-level specifications across AQA, Edexcel and OCR.
subjects.howItWorks: The tutor pins down the misconceptions, links each topic to exam-style questions, and rehearses the mark-scheme language examiners are looking for.

subjects.name: Modern languages
subjects.what: French, Spanish, German and more — building real speaking confidence alongside the grammar and vocabulary that exams ask for.
subjects.feels: Lots of talking. You'll practise out loud in a low-pressure setting, with grammar introduced as you need it rather than as dry rules.
subjects.origin: Speaking, listening, reading and writing across familiar topics, plus the grammar and tenses each level expects.
subjects.history: Aligned to GCSE and A-level modern-language specifications (AQA, Edexcel), including the speaking exam.
subjects.howItWorks: The tutor blends real conversation with targeted drills, corrects gently in the moment, and builds steadily toward each paper of the exam.

subjects.name: Music
subjects.what: Instrument lessons, theory and exam prep — for beginners finding their feet and graded students working toward the next certificate.
subjects.feels: Practical and encouraging. You'll play, the tutor listens and guides, and practice goals are set for the week ahead.
subjects.origin: Technique, sight-reading, aural, scales and repertoire — plus music theory for those who want it.
subjects.history: Geared to the graded systems (ABRSM, Trinity), from absolute beginner through to the advanced grades.
subjects.howItWorks: The tutor meets your level, breaks tricky passages down into manageable pieces, and shapes each lesson around your next goal or grade.

subjects.name: 11+, entrance & exam prep
subjects.what: Focused preparation for the high-stakes moments — 11+, independent-school entrance, GCSE and A-level finals — where technique and timing matter as much as content.
subjects.feels: Structured and reassuring. Past papers under realistic conditions, then a careful review of what to do differently next time.
subjects.origin: Verbal and non-verbal reasoning, subject content, timed practice, and the exam technique that turns knowledge into marks.
subjects.history: Tailored to the specific exam — GL and CEM 11+ styles, individual school papers, and the main GCSE and A-level boards.
subjects.howItWorks: The tutor builds a realistic plan back from the exam date, rehearses under timed conditions, and grows confidence so the day itself feels familiar.

faq.q: How do I find the right tutor?
faq.a: Tell us the subject and level you need and we'll match you with tutors who fit. You can read each tutor's profile, see their qualifications, and switch at any time if it isn't the right fit.
Lessons are booked and held through fair-do, including secure video for online tuition. Your tutor sets their own availability, and you'll get reminders before each lesson.

Tutors hold recognised teaching qualifications (such as QTS, PGCE or a relevant subject degree) and, where they work with children, an appropriate DBS check. Details are shown on each tutor's profile.
Tutors set their own fees, which you'll see before you book. See our pricing page for how the platform itself works.

for_tutors.hero_lead: fair-do is the calm, private software you use to run your own tutoring — your students, scheduling, secure video, payments and reminders, together in one place. On your computer and on your phone. Built for you, not for shareholders.
for_tutors.hero_note: Free to start · set up in ~15 minutes · keep your own students
for_tutors.features_subheading: A closer look at how fair-do takes the admin off your plate, so you can give your attention to the work that matters.

features.title: Your students stay yours
features.body: You own the relationship and the record — full stop. fair-do is the software you run your tutoring with, never a middleman who owns your students or rents them back to you. If you ever leave, your student list and your notes go with you.

features.title: Everything in one place
features.body: Your students, your schedule, secure video, payments and automatic reminders — all in a single calm place. No more stitching together a calendar, a video app, a card reader and a spreadsheet, and hoping they keep in step.

features.title: Genuinely simple
features.body: Set up in about 15 minutes, with no tech skills needed. Every page is written in plain English, with help right where you are — no manuals, no jargon. And because it installs as an app on your phone, you can run the whole thing from your pocket.

features.title: Private and secure
features.body: Everything is encrypted, with UK/EU data residency and appropriate safeguards for any services outside the UK. You stay in control: you are the data controller for your students' information, and fair-do is simply your processor. We never sell or share what your students trust you with.

features.title: Set your own prices
features.body: Your tutoring, your rates. Charge what you're worth, set a different price for a particular student if you like, or offer a package of lessons. You decide — we just make it easy to take the payment.

features.title: Get paid automatically
features.body: Students pay by card and the money lands in your bank through Stripe, about two business days after each lesson. No invoices to chase, no end-of-month reconciling. You keep what you charge — fair-do is a flat monthly subscription, not a cut of your lessons.

for_tutors.ethos_body: We believe tutors deserve better tools and fairer terms. fair-do only grows when you grow — so there's no lock-in, no hidden games, and no company getting rich off your hard work. Keep your students, keep your records, and leave whenever you like.

values.intro: We build software for a living. fair-do started because we kept noticing something about online tutoring that bothered us, and after a while we couldn't leave it alone.
values.bothered_p1: Good tutoring works. That's not a hunch — the evidence is strong, and most of us have either felt it or watched it change someone close to us.
values.bothered_p2: But the way it's sold online had drifted somewhere wrong. A lot of the big platforms take a huge share of every lesson — up to 65% in some cases. So the tutor, who trained for years and does the actual hard, human part of the work, ends up with maybe a third. The student pays full price, often when they're least able to afford it. And a company in the middle, who never meets either of them, takes the biggest cut.
values.bothered_p3: We kept thinking someone would come along and fix it. Nobody did. So we had a go ourselves.

beliefs.strong: Tutors should keep what they earn.
beliefs.body: On fair-do they keep what they charge — we don't take a cut of your lessons. Tutors pay one flat monthly fee to use the platform, and that's the arrangement. The work is theirs.

beliefs.strong: Cheaper isn't a gimmick — it's the point.
beliefs.body: Take out the middleman's cut and lessons can simply cost less. That's how good tutoring reaches families it currently prices out. We're not running a sale; this is the reason the company exists.

beliefs.strong: Nobody should get rich off the work tutors do.
beliefs.body: We charge a small, flat fee to keep things running, and that's it. A tutor's hard work isn't a number to be squeezed for margin, and we're not going to build a business that treats it like one.

beliefs.strong: We don't cut corners on trust.
beliefs.body: Every tutor here shares their teaching qualifications and a current DBS check. We check before anyone's approved, we keep a record of it, and we check again down the line. “Move fast and break things” is fine for a photo app. Not for this.

values.promise_tutors_intro: You've probably been burned by a platform before, so let's be specific.
promise_tutors_points: You keep what you charge — we don't take a percentage of your lessons. You cover only the standard card-processing fee every business pays, and we keep none of it.

The price you join at is the price you keep — for good. It doesn't go up. And as we grow, the rate for new joiners actually comes down, because a bigger platform should be cheaper to run, not greedier.

If you're just getting started — a couple of students, finding your feet — you start free. You don't pay anything until your tutoring has grown enough that fair-do is clearly worth it. You're verified and visible from day one regardless.
And it's your tutoring, not ours. Your rates, your hours, your students. We take care of the booking, the payments, the video. You can walk away whenever you like and take your students with you.
values.promise_students_intro: If you're the one looking for help, here's where we stand.
promise_students_points: Everyone you'll find here is a real, qualified tutor — we've checked.
You're not paying over the odds to cover someone's advertising budget, because there isn't a middleman taking a slice.
Your privacy matters more to us than almost anything. Lessons are encrypted. We keep only what we have to, we never sell your information, and we will never use your learning to sell you ads.
And you're a person to us. Not a conversion, not a number on a dashboard.
values.going_p1: Right now fair-do is mostly built for tutors. But the bigger idea is this: a place where anyone can find the right tutor for them and book them directly — the way you'd choose anyone you're trusting with something that matters. Good tutoring, fairly priced, as ordinary as it should be.
values.going_p2: We're building toward that slowly and in the right order. The verification, the safeguarding, the foundations — those come first, before we open things up. Some of it isn't here yet, and we'd rather say so than promise it early.
values.going_p3: The one thing that won't move is the idea underneath: tutoring that's fair. Fair to the people who do the work, and fair to the people who need it.
values.hello_body: We're a small team and we read everything — including the bits we've got wrong. Tell us.

compare.lead: An honest look at fair-do next to the platforms UK tutors typically consider. The marketplaces find you students — but they take 10–25% of every lesson. fair-do is the tool that lets you run your own practice, keep your own students, and earn what you actually charge.

edges.title: 0% commission on your lessons
edges.body: Tutorful takes 25%. MyTutor takes ~20%. Tutor Hunt takes 10–12.5%. fair-do takes nothing from your lessons — just a flat monthly plan.

edges.title: Your students stay yours
edges.body: On a marketplace, the platform owns the relationship. On fair-do, your students are yours — you hold the records, set the terms, and take them with you if you ever leave.

edges.title: Tools no marketplace offers
edges.body: AI lesson notes, interactive whiteboard, resource library, parent portal, group lessons, gift vouchers — all built in. No UK tutoring marketplace comes close.

edges.title: Free to start — no lock-in
edges.body: The Free plan runs your whole practice with no commission and no time limit. Upgrade to Pro for AI tools and analytics whenever you're ready.

compare.growing_intro: We believe in being straight with you:
compare.growing_newer_body: The marketplaces have years of tutor reviews and student reach. fair-do won't find you new students — it gives you far better tools to run the students you already have.
compare.growing_webapp_body: (it installs to your phone's home screen). A native iOS/Android app is in development.
compare.growing_fees_body: on payments — the trade for a genuinely free entry plan with no lesson commission.
compare.disclaimer: Comparison researched and fact-checked in June 2026 from each provider's own website and public reviews. Competitors' pricing and features change — please check their sites for the latest. This is an honest factual comparison.
compare.cta_sub: No commission · UK-first · your students stay yours

ai_tutoring.h1: Why not just use an AI tutor?
ai_tutoring.lead: It's a fair question — and the elephant in the room for a company like ours. We build software for human tutors, so we're not neutral. But you deserve a straight, impartial answer, not a sales pitch. Here's what AI can genuinely do, what it can't, and what the evidence actually says.
ai_tutoring.what_body: It usually means a chatbot or app that helps you learn — sometimes walking you through structured practice, sometimes just explaining a topic or checking your working. Some are purpose-built for education and curriculum-aware; others are general chatbots students use informally. They're available 24/7, usually free or cheap, and you can start instantly with no waiting list.

upsides.b: Free or a few pounds a month versus an hourly rate. No travel, no waiting list, available at 11pm the night before a deadline.
It never tires, never judges a "silly" question, and will re-explain the same thing ten different ways until it lands.
Practice questions, instant feedback, step-by-step worked solutions, flashcards and recall — AI does these consistently and tirelessly.
For a quick concept check, a homework nudge, or while waiting to start with a tutor, it can genuinely help a student take the first step.

risks.t: It doesn't actually know the student
risks.b: A good tutor reads a flicker of confusion on a face, remembers what clicked last week, and adapts in the moment. An AI guesses from text alone — it isn't really with the learner.
A real tutor builds a relationship with the family, can be asked how things are going, and is answerable for their work. A chatbot has none of that.
AI can give plausible but incorrect answers or working ("hallucinate"). For a student who can't yet tell right from wrong, that's a real problem.

risks.t: It can do the work for them
risks.b: The easiest path with a chatbot is to copy the answer. Real learning comes from struggling productively — something a good tutor protects and a chatbot can quietly undermine.
A child's schoolwork and details become data. Read the privacy policy carefully — some tools train on or share what you put in.
General AI doesn't reliably know your exact board, spec or mark scheme. A tutor who knows the exam can be the difference between knowing a topic and scoring the marks.

ai_tutoring.research_body: Honestly: it's early and mixed. AI tools can help with practice and feedback, and there's genuine promise in personalised drills. But the strongest evidence in education is still for structured, one-to-one teaching with a person who knows the learner. Long-term effects, accuracy at scale, and the impact on how children actually learn are still open questions, and professional bodies urge caution and human oversight.

research.label: Education Endowment Foundation — one-to-one tuition evidence
DfE — generative AI in education (policy paper)
EEF — using digital technology to improve learning

ai_tutoring.tools_note: Shared for information, not endorsement. Check each one's privacy policy, and remember none of them replaces a teacher who knows your child.

tools.name: Khan Academy (Khanmigo)
tools.note: An AI tutor built on Khan Academy's lessons; piloted in some schools for guided practice.

tools.name: Photomath
tools.note: Scans a maths problem and shows step-by-step working; handy for checking method.

tools.name: Duolingo
tools.note: Gamified language practice with AI-driven exercises and feedback.

tools.name: Century Tech
tools.note: An AI learning platform used in some UK schools for personalised practice.

tools.name: General LLMs (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini)
tools.note: Not built for teaching, but widely used to explain topics and check work. No guarantee of accuracy.

ai_tutoring.motivation_body: The heart of good tutoring isn't information — it's relationship. A tutor who believes in a student, notices the moment something clicks, and turns up week after week builds the confidence that makes learning stick. That encouragement, and the gentle accountability of someone who knows you, is where a lot of the progress happens. An AI can imitate the words. It can't choose to care, and it can't be proud of you. For drills and quick answers that's fine. For lasting confidence, it matters more than it sounds.
ai_tutoring.take_body: Use the right tool for the job. For practice questions, explanations, flashcards, or an 11pm moment when no one's awake, a good AI tool can genuinely help — and we'd rather a student have that than nothing. For building real understanding, confidence and exam technique, we believe a real, qualified human tutor is worth it — and we've built fair-do so that's affordable, not a luxury. The two aren't enemies. But they're not the same.
ai_tutoring.disclaimer: This article is general information, not formal academic advice. Links to third-party tools and research are for context, not endorsement.

gift.gentle_body: Learning is personal, and being given lessons can feel complicated. We never tell the recipient it's "because they need help" — just that someone believes in them. They redeem privately, choose their own tutor, and are never pressured to use it. If they never do, that's okay too.

gift.secure_note: Secure payment via Stripe. Vouchers never expire.

onboarding_home.heading: Welcome to fair-do
onboarding_home.subtitle: How are you joining us today?
onboarding_home.student_subtitle_need: Find qualified tutors at fair prices
onboarding_home.student_subtitle_have: Connect with your tutor on fair-do
onboarding_home.teacher_subtitle: Run your whole studio from one place
onboarding_home.footer_note: You can only choose once. Contact support to change your role.

onboarding_complete.heading: Application submitted
onboarding_complete.review_pre: Your profile is under review. We’ll email you within 2 business days once your

onboarding_connect.heading: Join your tutor
onboarding_connect.lead: On fair-do, you connect directly to your own tutor — there's no middleman.
onboarding_connect.invite_body: Open the personal link your tutor sent you, or scan their QR code. That connects you and lets you book — no browsing required.
onboarding_connect.no_invite_body: Ask your tutor for your fair-do invite — that's how students join for now. Browsing tutors yourself is coming soon.

onboarding_student.step1_subtitle: Two quick steps. Take your time.
onboarding_student.about_prompt: Want to know who we are first?
onboarding_student.subjects_label: What subject(s) do you need help with?
onboarding_student.levels_label: What level are you studying at?
onboarding_student.frequency_label: How often would you like lessons?
onboarding_student.step2_subtitle: One thing we need you to know.
onboarding_student.consent_body: We use your details (name, email, lesson history and progress notes) only to match you with a tutor and run your lessons. Seen by your tutor and essential staff only. Full details in our
onboarding_student.consent_checkbox: I consent to fair-do processing my personal data to match me with a tutor and provide tuition.
onboarding_student.error_generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.

onboarding_teacher.error_generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
onboarding_teacher.step1_subtitle: Students see this when browsing tutors — a photo and a clear title help them choose you.
onboarding_teacher.photo_hint: Optional now — but profiles with a photo get far more enquiries. You can change it any time.
onboarding_teacher.professional_title_placeholder: e.g. Qualified Maths Teacher, GCSE Science Tutor
onboarding_teacher.tagline_placeholder: One warm line students see first
onboarding_teacher.bio_placeholder: Describe your teaching approach, experience, and what students can expect…
onboarding_teacher.rate_note_post: by card — paid straight to your connected account. fair-do charges your monthly plan plus a small card commission.
onboarding_teacher.step2_subtitle: Verified before your profile goes live.
onboarding_teacher.license_state_hint: You can only see students located in this state. fair-do is live in New York first.
onboarding_teacher.step4_subtitle: fair-do pays you via Stripe. Setup takes ~5 minutes.
onboarding_teacher.payment_note_pre: Students pay by card via Stripe and the money goes straight to your connected account. fair-do's fee is your monthly plan plus a small card commission — see
onboarding_teacher.payment_kyc_note: Payments typically reach your bank around 2 business days after each lesson. Stripe handles all KYC.
onboarding_teacher.agreement_pre: I have read and accept the
onboarding_teacher.agreement_post: . I understand I am the data controller for my students' records and fair-do is my processor.

teacher_dashboard.credential_paused_body: Renew below to take new bookings again — sessions already booked are unaffected.
teacher_dashboard.payments_not_connected_body: Complete Stripe setup to receive payouts.

getting_started.heading: Getting started
getting_started.intro: A few quick steps and you're ready to see students. We'll guide you through each one.

recurring_card_prompt.title: Keep your regular lessons running
recurring_card_prompt.description: Your tutor set up a standing lesson. Authorise a card and each one is booked and paid automatically — cancel any time.

booking_link_card.heading: 🔗 Your booking link
booking_link_card.description: Share this so students can book you directly — add it to your website, email signature or social bio.

calendar_sync.heading: 📅 Sync your calendar
calendar_sync.subtitle: See your fair-do lessons in Google, Apple or Outlook — they update automatically.
calendar_sync.apple_instructions: , or Settings → Calendar → Add Account → Subscribed Calendar → paste the link.
calendar_sync.google_instructions: Other calendars → + → From URL → paste the link.
calendar_sync.outlook_instructions: Add calendar → Subscribe from web → paste the link.
calendar_sync.privacy_note: Keep this link private — anyone with it can see your lesson times.

install_hint.heading: Add fair-do to your phone
install_hint.ios_suffix: — fair-do opens like an app, no browser needed.
install_hint.android_body: Install it as an app for one-tap access — no app store needed.

push_toggle.title: 🔔 Notifications
push_toggle.status_on: On — we'll alert you to new messages and bookings.
push_toggle.status_off: Get alerted when a student messages or books, even when fair-do is closed.

welcome_modal.intro: fair-do is your whole practice in one place. Here's the gist — it takes a few minutes to set up.

steps.title: Add your students.
steps.body: Invite them by email, or import your whole list. They're yours — no one else sees them.

steps.title: Book lessons.
steps.body: Pick a time; we create a private video room and email your student automatically.

steps.title: Get paid.
steps.body: Connect payments when you're ready and the money lands in your bank automatically.

welcome_modal.help_before: Stuck at any point? There's a
welcome_modal.help_after: link at the top of every page — and no question is too small.

recurring_booking_card.status_waiting: waiting for student to add a card
recurring_booking_card.footer_note: The student gets a prompt to authorise a card. We then create and charge each lesson automatically.

teacher_approved.subject: You're approved — welcome to fair-do
teacher_approved.heading: Welcome to fair-do, {firstName}
teacher_approved.preheader: Your profile is live — students can book you now.
teacher_approved.body_p1: Your {qualificationBody} credentials have been verified, and your profile is now live. Students can book lessons with you.
teacher_approved.body_p2: You keep what you charge — we take no commission on your own students. Payouts reach your bank 2 business days after each completed lesson.

gift_voucher.preheader_gift: {amount} of tuition, on someone who cares.
gift_voucher.intro_receipt: Your {amount} tuition voucher is ready. Share this code with your recipient:
gift_voucher.intro_gift: Someone wants to help you learn. Here's {amount} of tuition credit, on them.

session_reminder.subject: Reminder: your lesson tomorrow — {date}
session_reminder.heading: See you soon, {firstName}
session_reminder.preheader: Your lesson with {displayName} is coming up.
session_reminder.body_p1: Your lesson with {displayName} is on {date}.
session_reminder.body_p3: Can't make it? {cancelLink} — cancellations within 24 hours are non-refundable.

home.title: fair-do — your whole tutoring business, in one calm place
home.description: The simple, private tool for independent tutors: your students, scheduling, secure video and payments in one place. Set up in minutes, run it from your phone.

pricing.title: Pricing — fair-do
pricing.description: Simple, fair pricing for your tutoring practice. Start free, pay as you grow. Founding pricing locked for early tutors.

about.title: About — fair-do
about.description: We are technologists with a heart. Tutors should earn enough to focus on teaching, good tuition should reach more people, and no company should get rich off the work tutors do.

faq.title: FAQ — fair-do
faq.description: Common questions about fair-do: what it is, pricing, student ownership, data security, and getting set up as a tutor.

for_tutors.title: How fair-do works — the calm tool for independent tutors
for_tutors.description: See how fair-do helps you run your whole tutoring business — your own students, scheduling, secure video, payments and reminders, on web and phone. Private, simple, and fair. Start free.

for_schools.title: fair-do for Schools — managed tutoring for your students
for_schools.description: Give your pupils access to verified UK tutors. Transparent per-lesson pricing, no per-seat lock-in, simple invoicing.

compare.title: How fair-do compares — software for UK tutors
compare.description: An honest, factual comparison of fair-do with the booking and admin tools UK tutors usually weigh up — for independent private tutors.

ai_tutoring.title: AI tutoring: an honest look — fair-do
ai_tutoring.description: An impartial guide to AI tutors and learning chatbots — the benefits, the real risks, what the research says, the tools available, and where a human teacher still matters.

values.title: Why we built fair-do — our values
values.description: Tutoring that is fair — to the people who do the work, and the people who need it. Why fair-do exists, and what we promise tutors and students.

styles.title: Subjects & how tutoring works — fair-do
styles.description: Maths, English, the sciences, languages, music and exam prep explained — what you'll cover, how lessons work, the exams they map to, and where to read more.

help.title: Help & FAQs — fair-do
help.description: Find your way around fair-do — how tutoring works, pricing, finding a tutor, and how to get in touch.

pricing_parents.title: Parent portal pricing — fair-do
pricing_parents.description: Follow your child's tutoring — lessons, notes, messages and invoices, all in one calm place. £4.99/month, one price for the whole family.
